Overview: Start with a validation question that can fail in a short playtest, then cut until the answer lives inside one honest loop.
Failure Patterns and Fixes: If the question cannot kill the concept, the pack is still pitching instead of proving.

Overview: Do not explain the whole product vision here; name the proof target and the cut line.
Output Format: Keep the output field list fillable and remove any line that does not change kill, greenlight, or redesign.

Quality Checks: Check whether the validation question can fail fast enough to trigger a redesign.
Check whether the smallest honest loop is playable after art, meta systems, and comfort features are stripped away.
Check whether the feature cut removes attractive work instead of protecting comfort.
Check whether the out-of-scope list blocks creep and keeps a clear kill list.
Failure Patterns and Fixes: If support systems keep sneaking back in, move them to out of scope with a condition for re-entry and keep a kill list so this does not become a dream-expanding skill.
Keep the failure pass anchored on scope creep, vertical slice drift, mood instead of loop, and content hiding uncertainty before approving the first playable.

Output Format: Keep the field list explicit: validation goal, minimum honest loop, core features, minimum content scope, required systems, explicitly out of scope, main production risks, and build recommendation.
